subject: general
title: Student with intellectual disability — much slower than classmates
query_text: Student with intellectual disability — much slower than classmates

immediate_steps.title: Immediate Action
steps: Seat the student near you or a patient peer — proximity reduces anxiety and allows quiet check-ins without singling them out
Break every task into one small step at a time; give the next step only after the first is done — this prevents overwhelm
Use a simple visual cue (a drawn tick or star on their page) to signal 'you did it' — immediate positive feedback builds confidence

classroom_activity.title: Classroom Activity — Touch & Tell
classroom_activity.activity: While the class works on a reading or writing task, give this student a small object (stone, eraser, leaf). Ask them to describe it using 3 words — shape, size, feel. They can whisper to you or a buddy. This mirrors the NCERT Class 4 'Let us Speak' tactile activity and keeps them meaningfully engaged at their own level without isolation.
classroom_activity.materials: Any small classroom object — eraser, stone, chalk piece, leaf

inclusion_strategy.title: Inclusion Strategy
inclusion_strategy.strategy: Use a 'buddy pair' system — pair this student with a calm, kind classmate for structured tasks. The buddy does not teach; they simply work alongside. This reduces the student's isolation and builds belonging. Assign the buddy a rotating role so it feels like a classroom honour, not a burden.

reflection_prompt.title: Reflection
reflection_prompt.question: After today's class, did this student complete at least one task fully — however small? If yes, that is a real learning moment worth building on tomorrow.

source_citation.title: Source
source_citation.reference: NEP 2020, Section 7.5(d) — cooperation across schools for education of children with disabilities; NCERT Class 4 English 'Santoor' — tactile learning activity (Let us Do, p.64); NISHTHA Module — Inclusive Education practices
